title: Fraxinella alba

intro:

Fraxinus alba, commonly known as White Ash, is a deciduous tree native to Eastern North America. The health benefits of White Ash include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of White Ash include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with White Ash include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions.
